assertion_results.text: Version impact table shows NO (not affected) for all supported versions — every version ships a dependency version outside the CVE's affected range
assertion_results.evidence: version-impact.md contains a table with all 7 versions (2.1.0, 2.1.1, 2.2.0, 2.2.1, 2.2.2, 2.2.3, 2.2.4) each showing 'NO' in the 'Affected?' column. Each version ships serde_json >= 1.0.137, which is above the fix threshold of 1.0.135. The summary states: 'No supported versions are affected. Every version across both the 2.1.x and 2.2.x streams ships serde_json >= 1.0.137, which is above the fix threshold of 1.0.135.'

assertion_results.text: Triage outcome recommends closing as Not a Bug with resolution, not creating remediation tasks (Step 8 Case C)
assertion_results.evidence: triage-outcome.md states 'Decision: Case C -- No Supported Versions Affected' and 'Recommendation: Close as "Not a Bug" (not affected).' Section 3 of Proposed Jira Actions explicitly says 'Close as Not a Bug (Step 8, Case C)' with action 'Transition TC-8002 to Closed with resolution "Not a Bug"'. The final section states 'No Remediation Tasks Needed -- Since no supported versions are affected (Case C), no remediation tasks are created.'

assertion_results.text: The close recommendation includes a comment documenting the version impact evidence — listing each version and its dependency version as proof of non-impact
assertion_results.evidence: triage-outcome.md section 3 includes an 'Add comment' action containing a table listing each version with its serde_json version: 2.1.0/1.0.137, 2.1.1/1.0.137, 2.2.0/1.0.138, 2.2.1/1.0.138, 2.2.2/-- (retag of 2.2.1), 2.2.3/1.0.139, 2.2.4/1.0.139, all marked NO for Affected?. The comment also explains: 'All supported versions ship serde_json >= 1.0.137, which is outside the affected range (versions before 1.0.135).'

assertion_results.text: VEX Justification is set to 'Component not Present' or appropriate value when the VEX field is configured (Step 8 VEX Justification)
assertion_results.evidence: triage-outcome.md section 3 states: 'Set VEX Justification (customfield_12345) to "Component not Present"'. The VEX field is configured as shown in data-extraction.md (VEX Justification custom field: customfield_12345). The output also notes an alternative 'Vulnerable Code not Present' could apply, but defaults to 'Component not Present' per skill procedure.

assertion_results.text: The analysis does NOT create any remediation tasks — no upstream backport, no downstream propagation (Step 8 Case C closes the issue)
assertion_results.evidence: triage-outcome.md has a dedicated section 'No Remediation Tasks Needed' stating: 'Since no supported versions are affected (Case C), no remediation tasks are created. No upstream backport tasks or downstream propagation tasks are necessary.' No remediation task creation appears anywhere in the Proposed Jira Actions. The only actions are assign/transition, affects versions correction, close as Not a Bug, add label, and post-triage summary comment.
